rootstock.title: About Rootstock
rootstock.paragraph1: In 2015, when most people were still debating whether Bitcoin could support smart contracts, Rootstock chose action. We believe Bitcoin is not just digital gold, but should become a complete financial infrastructure.
rootstock.paragraph2: Today, the Rootstock mainnet has been running for 3000 days. In these 3000 days, we have had no major security incidents, no downtime, and have steadily provided services to users. This persistence comes from our belief in technology and support from the community.
rootstock.paragraph3: Rootstock uses merged mining with Bitcoin, giving smart contracts the same level of security as Bitcoin. Developers can build DeFi protocols and decentralized financial applications here, while users can experience the full possibilities of Web3 without leaving the Bitcoin ecosystem.

whatIsRootstock.title: What is Rootstock?
whatIsRootstock.paragraph1: Rootstock (RSK) is the most secure smart contract platform in the world, secured by the Bitcoin network's immense hashpower. Launched on January 16, 2018, Rootstock has been pioneering Bitcoin DeFi for over 3000 days.
whatIsRootstock.paragraph2: This commemorative Soul Bound Token celebrates this milestone and honors everyone who has been part of this journey. By minting this SBT, you become a permanent part of Rootstock's history.

q1.question: What is a Soul Bound Token?
q1.answer: A Soul Bound Token (SBT) is a non-transferable NFT that is permanently bound to your wallet address. It cannot be sold, transferred, or given away. It serves as a permanent proof of participation.

q2.question: How much does it cost to mint?
q2.answer: Minting is completely free! You only need to pay the network gas fee, which is typically very low on Rootstock. The gas fee goes to the network validators, not to us.

q3.question: Can I mint more than one?
q3.answer: No, each wallet address can only mint one SBT. This ensures fair distribution and maintains the commemorative nature of the token.

q4.question: What network should I use?
q4.answer: You need to connect to the Rootstock network (Chain ID 30 for mainnet, or 31 for testnet). Make sure your wallet is configured with the correct network before minting.
